reviews.review_description:
I am not really a dancer, but I tried this game and it was an easy way to learn some moves! It’s cool being able to have a virtual partner to teach me the ropes! Definitely recommend if you have never danced and want to practice alone before moving on to the dance floor.

There were a couple of bugs, but they were very easy to overlook and fixed themselves quickly. Overall great experience!

reviews.review_title: 💃✨ Lead the Dance, Own the Floor
reviews.review_description:
Trip the Light is a refreshing VR dancing game where you lead the dance, master the rhythm, and truly own the floor.

The game offers two modes: VR and Mixed Reality. In one mode, you follow and copy the stylish movements of a model, keeping up with the rhythm and improving your coordination. In the other, you can dance with a partner, which adds a fun and social twist to the experience.

There aren’t many dancing games available in VR, and this one is well executed, responsive, and genuinely fun to play. If you enjoy rhythm games or simply want a new way to move and have fun in VR, this is definitely worth trying. 💃🎶

reviews.review_title: Great for casual dancing or beginners!
reviews.review_description: For both seasoned dancers and dance noobs this game does a lovely job catering to anyone with a love of dance. It mixes the matching aspect of games like Just Dance and DDR with actual partner dancing and it’s a ton of fun. The song are banger too and will get stuck in your head!!

reviews.review_title: The fun VR game with so much potential!
reviews.review_description:
TLDR: This is a really fun VR game with so much potential even at its early state! I mostly enjoyed the rhythm challenge mode since it matches my style, but there are some issues in the game that needs tweaking, nothing game-breaking and mostly nitpicks, just hoping they keep expanding on the game and adding more modes and features.

The Nitpicks: Sometimes the partners get uncomfortably close during the partner dance mode and holding hands relies solely on controller proximity which leads to accidental grabs, make it more like pressing the trigger on the controllers do a light hold, while pressing down on the grab button have a stronger hold, and if no triggers are pressed, then don’t grab instead of guessing if you want to grab the partner or not. Menu navigation is also a bit hard, relying only on physical placement without trigger inputs, plus there are fewer features compared to competitors like foot layout feedback with headset tracking for dance accuracy and hand tracking, but that’s not a make it or break it for me, just some things to look out for. 

Bugs (This is meant to help the devs, not to talk down on the game): Sometimes during the rhythm game mode, the model highlight for the next move sometimes ends up in the ground at the very end of the song. Sometime the partner’s model clips through the clothing during the partner dance. Sometimes during the rhythm game the game flags the moves that are correct to be incorrect (though that can be me just being bad, but I noticed that in some of the songs). During the partner dance, when you’re trying to spin the partner around by putting your hand above and spinning them around, sometimes it doesn’t register or doesn’t full complete. 

But all in all, this is a great game for people who want to dance or just vibe in a virtual environment with a virtual partner and learn how to dance!

Like so many other reviews, I too have two left feet. This was surprisingly simple, fast to pick up and FUN! I’m not sure if there are other dancing experiences ( I’m coming from Dance Dance Revolution back in the 2000’s) but this has to be the best dancing experience so far on any platform. I’m looking forward to future updates and the experience and my dancing moves getting better. The goal is to dance with my wife more, if she finds out about this, she might not wait for me to get better and just use this 😅
So far, learned the timing of basic salsa step and when to turn. Partner dance mode surprisingly does make you want to practice more than if you were alone, and rhythm mode is like Just Dance for VR!

I have played so many dance games over the years - first Just Dance on the wii, then held on to my connect for a long time. Dance Central and others are long time favorites.

I also was on a dance team in real life, so this type of game speaks to the heart! 

This has two awesome components - the first is the rhythm mode. This is such a wide appeal, and I really like the way the patterns were done. Easy and enjoyable.

The partner dance feels magical - this is where the tech in VR is amazing today and speaks to the future. It feels like a safer space to learn partner dance.

The Salsa is intriguing, would definitely like to see more of this in the future.

Definitely recommend this title whether you are looking for just a fun, get up and moving dance game or something where you can pick up new skills.

reviews.review_title: The dance game I didn’t know I needed
reviews.review_description: This signals the beginning of a VR I hoped for - truly embodied with little interactions like on the dance floor social dancing that might surprise you. Only 2 months out with steady updates: new songs, new moves. I can’t wait for what’s coming next. Bravi team!

This is the smoothest and coolest dance game on VR!  It does a lot of things really well and is really fun!  Way more than a salsa tutorial.  The music is a lot better than the other salsa apps I tried, it has real sound track not just basic sounding latin songs designed for lessons only 

The partner dancing is very impressive and the match me part is really cool with Just Dance vibes but more interactive and responsive. The salsa lesson is also cool, I'm really learning the steps from this!
Growing up with dancing games for the xbox at friends' houses, it's been a lot of fun trying one out in VR! Not only do they have song routines to dance along with, you can also learn to salsa!

reviews.review_title: So much fun learning the moves!
reviews.review_description:
Loved doing the rhythm challenge! I haven’t danced in awhile so it was fun learning new moves even though I was slow to react and remember them. I love that you can do it at home while no one’s watching! lol 

It was fun to challenge myself to master the moves so I played the same one 3x in a row and my accuracy increased by 3 -5 % each time! I started to learn the salsa steps which I’d like to master and then try them out IRL.  Highly recommend getting this app!

I almost went to college for dance.  But I NEVER learned how to dance with a partner.  This game has patience for those of us who need to learn how to lead and follow.  My daughter wants to learn different styles of dance for her theatre "career".  This would be a great way for her to learn.
Five stars ✨

I don’t know to dance but this seems pretty cool. Very smooth, I like how you can dance with an actual partner. Had a lot fun with the rhythm challenge, that was very cool. Up to par with many other games on the meta environment! 

9.8/10, would recommend

I’m not much of a dancer, and I don’t usually play dance games, but this game was so much fun to play! Being able to dance so closely with a partner helped me feel a little more familiar and confident with dancing like those As someone who has never done salsa before the lesson was informative and concise in its teachings. Match Me was my favorite mode - it was high energy, a good balance of fun and genuine challenge. It was much more of my style!

This experience feels like the beginning of something truly special! Potentially the defining dance game in VR. It strikes a brilliant balance between authentic dance instruction and genuinely engaging, replayable gameplay. Even in this early state, the foundation is remarkably solid, and there's already so much to appreciate.

What's most exciting is the untapped potential: imagine more precise motion tracking, richer real-time feedback, and increasingly sophisticated choreography that really develops your dance skills. The soundtrack is awesome and elevates every moment of the experience.

I can't wait to see where this goes. This feels like the beginning of something transformative.

The way the partners react is amazing: you guide their hand, you link a step, you attempt a more ambitious move, and everything remains fluid and intuitive. It creates a great atmosphere, even in your living room!
 
Between the training modes, the "Match Me" style solo choreography, and the announced future styles, you can feel the desire to make dance accessible, fun, and rewarding.
The MR/VR transition adds a real thrill, as if you're traveling with your partner from one setting to another. 
 
Trip The Light manages to make learning fun, accessible, and rewarding.
It's a dance companion that puts you in a good mood and makes you want to progress day after day, step by step!

I loved this game! I feel like after just 30 minutes playing time I already had the hang of the salsa. And I am NOT a dancer. Can't wait to see what other dance styles they come out with next. Overall, a super fun game and impressively realistic. This is one I'll play again and again.

It has potential.  I like it more as a learning tool than to free dance which feels weird. I mean it's cool, but I enjoyed the Salsa lesson portion of it more as it had guidance.  Price point is hard to beat, I'll keep it and see what it does.  In my time in VR, devs seem to abandon these games after a bit.  I hate that the stars don't show right.  I gave it 3 stars, not 5.

developer_response.body:
Great feedback, thank you for trying the game R2-3PO!  Really appreciate it! :-)

Could you provided specifics on what you mean by "the stars don't show right", so that we may investigate?

Don't worry, we're just getting started and have lots of cool stuff coming!

reviews.review_description: This is the most fun I’ve ever had while “exercising”. I normally have two left feet, but the stunning visuals helped guide me along in such a non judgmental way. Encouraged me to feel like I could dance anywhere.  The energetic and vibrant music with dance instructions baked into the lyrics really help too! Intuitive interface and easy to navigate through the settings. Was leveling up through difficulty stages in no time. Could see this game being a hit with all ages and levels of experience. Who doesn’t love dancing?!

I understand the basics of salsa now! Not overwhelming and just gets right to it - plus the surprise environment changes as you spin your partner is a fun touch! Looking forward to seeing this expand with even more dance styles…
Been playing it with my son, good times. Would recommend.

This is my first VR game and I'm blown away.  It's so intuitive to use, and REALLY fun!!  I can't believe how realistic the partner dancing is, and I feel like I'm learning rapidly.  The music is great, the instructor is helpful, and the visuals are spectacular.  I'll be spending a lot of time on this - can't wait!

I was sweating so much when I played Move Your Body in match me. There is also scoring! It was so sad when my friend beat me by 80k poimts:(

Partner dance was more chill and had more songs too. It allowed me to dance however I like and the partner follows me well and keeps talking to me.

Salsa lesson was just one lesson so I'm waiting to see what content they in the future.

Tried the early version at the trade shows - love from the first sight!
Now its full game cannot stop playing, entire family now fell in love.

PS cant wait for store to open, please add more outfits!! 🙏

Don't let yourself be that awkward dude at the party standing in the corner 😂 this game showed me how much rhythm i don't have and helps fix you. The rhythm challenge is basically Just Dance also really fun

Yeah, I don’t consider myself a dancer and often feel a little bit intimidated - almost like an imposter on the dance floor.

That’s what makes Trip the Light special - it’s a safe place to practice and not feel to self conscious. 

I like the direction the Early Access game is going. I especially like their preview of learning salsa. 💃 

They’ve done a great job and excited for new updates.

I really like the partner dancing and the animation style. It’s really pretty! I’m not usually in the Lead position, so that was totally new. But I was able to do a bunch of turns with cool effects. The part with trying to match the moves has a couple super easy ones that are fun, and…I’ll have to practice the hard one! LOL!

reviews.review_title: Rising to the challenge or Hubris in VR
reviews.review_description: I am often underwhelmed by games that claim to be movement games. So I took on the highest difficulty in Match Me and was gasping halfway through. This game is the right amount of challenge and pretty. I am so excited to keep rising to the challenges.

This game is really ambitious, nobody else that I can see is doing something like it. Some dance modes resemble other games, but other parts are completely new. And there is a lot of detail packed into all of it. Can't wait to see where it goes from here.

reviews.review_title: Just what i was hoping for!
reviews.review_description: I got out my old quest 2 just for this game, been watching this project since the Kickstarter.  It's as good as I was hoping! Can't wait for more updates!  🤩🤩
Oculus should have launched with a dancing game as engaging as this. I got to dancing quickly. Great game and I hope it grows into beatssaber.
how about the waltz, foxtrot, two step

developer_response.body: We agree and are working on a better foot placement interface before we bring those in.

reviews.review_description: then I got this app and now I have a left & right!  Thanks Dark Arts!
Been waiting a really long time for this game to come out!  The music is sooooo good!  It's a work out and looks so good!
Wow I can actually hold hands and dance with my partner!!
